title: Outdoor Laser Safety & Airspace Assessment
description: Assessment covering FAA regulations, NOTAM procedures, outdoor hazard calculations, and international outdoor standards.

questions.question: What is the US federal law that makes pointing a laser at an aircraft a crime?
questions.explanation: 18 USC Section 39A makes it a federal felony to knowingly aim a laser pointer at an aircraft or its flight path. Penalties can include up to 5 years imprisonment.

questions.question: How far in advance must a NOTAM typically be filed for outdoor laser operations?
questions.explanation: NOTAMs for outdoor laser operations should generally be filed 24-72 hours in advance to allow proper distribution and processing through the Notice to Air Missions system.

questions.question: What does ARTCC stand for in FAA context?
options: Aviation Risk and Technology Compliance Center
Airspace Regulation and Traffic Control Coordination
questions.explanation: ARTCC (Air Route Traffic Control Center) manages en route air traffic in designated airspace. Laser show operators with complex outdoor shows should coordinate with their local ARTCC.

questions.question: Compared to indoor calculations, outdoor NOHD calculations should account for:
options: Atmospheric transmission losses and beam degradation
questions.explanation: Outdoor NOHD calculations must account for atmospheric effects — humidity, aerosols, and turbulence all attenuate or scatter the beam, affecting hazard distances. This often (but not always) reduces the NOHD.

questions.question: What does IEC 60825-3 specifically address?
questions.explanation: IEC 60825-3 (Safety of laser products — Part 3: Guidance for laser displays) specifically addresses safety requirements for laser light shows, displays, and entertainment applications.
